reasoning: Isosporiasis is a neglected protozoan infection (caused by Cystoisospora belli) primarily affecting immunocompromised patients. It is treated with anti-protozoal medications like TMP-SMX. There is currently no vaccine available or in late-stage clinical trials, and virtually zero commercial incentive to develop one. Developing a human or animal vaccine for a protozoan parasite typically takes decades. The likelihood of a real vaccine being developed, approved, and added to this Wikipedia page within the next 6 months is negligible. The only real avenue for a 'Yes' resolution would be an erroneous Wikipedia edit (vandalism) that remains uncorrected on the resolution dates.
